description_long: This traffic barricade consists of two sturdy vertical posts with wide, circular bases for stability, supporting three horizontal slats. The slats alternate between bright orange and gray stripes, creating a highly visible pattern commonly used for roadwork or restricted access areas. The posts and bases are a muted gray and orange, with the tops of the posts capped in orange for added visibility. The structure appears to be made primarily of plastic or composite material, designed to be lightweight yet durable. The horizontal slats are evenly spaced and securely attached to the posts, forming a solid barrier. There are no visible warning signs or lights, and the surface finish is matte with a slightly rough texture for grip.
description: A highly visible orange and gray striped traffic barricade with two vertical posts and three horizontal slats, supported by wide circular bases.
description_view_0: Side view showing the narrow profile of the barricade, with both posts and the edges of the slats visible.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the full width of the barricade, with three alternating orange and gray horizontal slats and both support posts.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the slim profile and the alignment of the posts and slats.
description_view_3: Rear view mirroring the front, showing the three striped slats and both support posts with circular bases.
attribute_reflective_striping_pattern: Horizontal stripes alternating orange and gray, each stripe of equal width, repeated across all three slats.
